output.summary: Review and fix the Text block - a two-column layout with ThemeHeading (sticky) on the left and InnerBlocks rich text on the right. Three bugs found: missing `not-discourse` class in TSX, missing `$children` docblock annotation, and no `hasContent` guard in TSX matching the PHP conditional. Create test page with 7 variations covering all scenarios.

accessibilityReview.currentState: The Text block has reasonable baseline accessibility: it uses a semantic <section> element via useBlockProps/theme_block_props. The theme_block_props() helper automatically adds aria-labelledby pointing to the heading ID generated by ThemeHeading via theme_block_region_id() when a heading attribute exists. The block uses proper heading hierarchy (ThemeHeading h2 on left, h3 subheadings allowed in InnerBlocks on right). The discourse wrapper provides proper text styling. InnerBlocks supports semantic content types (paragraphs, headings, lists, quotes, separators). Buttons in ThemeHeading render as proper <a> elements with accessible labels.
issues: No critical accessibility issues found. The block is a static content layout without interactive elements requiring ARIA patterns.

Minor: The sticky positioning (md:sticky md:top-8) on the ThemeHeading column could potentially cause content to overlap on very small viewport heights, but this is a progressive enhancement that degrades gracefully.
recommendations: No ARIA pattern changes needed. The block is purely content-based (no tabs, accordions, carousels, or other interactive widgets).
The theme's automatic aria-labelledby on the section element via theme_block_props() provides proper landmark labeling when a heading is present.
Heading hierarchy is maintained: ThemeHeading uses h2 (headingSize=2), InnerBlocks template starts with h3.
No keyboard navigation changes needed - all content is static and natively accessible.

bugReport.description: TSX wrapper section is missing the `not-discourse` class that exists in PHP. The PHP template has `theme_block_props('text-block py-10 sm:py-16 not-discourse')` but the TSX has `className: 'text-block py-10 sm:py-16'`. The `not-discourse` class prevents the discourse styling from bleeding into the section wrapper. Without it, the editor rendering may have incorrect text styling on elements outside the `.discourse` div.
bugReport.fix: Add `not-discourse` to the useBlockProps className: `className: 'text-block py-10 sm:py-16 not-discourse'`

bugReport.description: TSX does not wrap ThemeHeading in a `hasContent` conditional like PHP does. In PHP, `$hasContent` checks whether eyebrow, heading, description, or buttons have values, and only renders ThemeHeading when true. In TSX, ThemeHeading always renders regardless of content. This means the two-column grid always has two columns in the editor even when the left column should be hidden, creating a TSX/PHP rendering mismatch on the frontend.
bugReport.fix: The TSX should keep ThemeHeading always visible in the editor (for authoring UX - users need to be able to fill in content). However, we should use `shouldDisplay()` to conditionally show/hide the ThemeHeading when the block is not selected and has no content. Import `shouldDisplay` from `@taktdev/utilities` and wrap ThemeHeading in `{ (displayContent || hasContent) && ... }` where `hasContent` mirrors the PHP logic. This ensures the editor preview matches the frontend while still allowing content entry.

bugReport.description: PHP template is missing @var docblock annotation for `$children` variable. While the validate_sync tool passed (it may not check for this specifically), the convention from the generic block type doc and other blocks like ContentWithMedia shows that `$children` should be documented.
bugReport.fix: Add `@var string $children Inner blocks content` and `@var WP_Block $block Block instance` docblock annotations to the PHP template.

fileChanges.description: Add `not-discourse` class to section wrapper and add shouldDisplay/hasContent conditional for ThemeHeading
fileChanges.details: 1. Add `not-discourse` to useBlockProps className to match PHP. 2. Import `shouldDisplay` and `cn` from '@taktdev/utilities'. 3. Compute `hasContent` boolean matching PHP logic (eyebrow || heading || description || buttons with url/postId). 4. Wrap ThemeHeading in `{ (displayContent || hasContent) && ... }` conditional.

fileChanges.description: Add docblock annotations for $children and $block variables
fileChanges.details: Add PHP docblock comment at the top of the file documenting all available variables including $children and $block, following the pattern from ContentWithMedia.php.

variations.demoDescription: All fields populated: eyebrow, heading, description, buttons, and rich text inner blocks with heading, paragraphs, and list.
variations.purpose: Verify complete rendering with all ThemeHeading fields and diverse inner blocks

variations.demoDescription: Only the heading is provided. All other ThemeHeading fields and inner blocks are empty. Tests hasContent conditional rendering.
variations.purpose: Test minimal ThemeHeading with only heading populated

variations.demoDescription: No eyebrow, heading, description, or buttons. Only inner blocks are present. Tests that the ThemeHeading column is hidden when hasContent is false.
variations.purpose: Verify PHP hasContent conditional hides ThemeHeading column when empty

variations.demoDescription: Very long inner block content to test the sticky positioning of the ThemeHeading column. The left column should stick to the top while scrolling through the right column.
variations.purpose: Test md:sticky md:top-8 behavior with extended content

variations.demoTitle: Eyebrow and Heading Only - No Description or Buttons
variations.demoDescription: Tests conditional display with eyebrow and heading but no description or buttons. Verifies partial ThemeHeading rendering.
Tests the anchor/ID support for deep linking. The section should have id='test-anchor' in the rendered HTML.
variations.purpose: Verify anchor attribute is applied to the section element

variations.demoTitle: Rich Inner Blocks - Multiple Content Types
variations.demoDescription: Tests inner blocks with diverse content types: headings, paragraphs, lists, quotes, and separators.
variations.purpose: Verify all allowed inner block types render correctly within the discourse wrapper

designDecisions.question: Should the block add a fallback aria-label when no heading is present (for the 'No ThemeHeading Content' case)?
designDecisions.recommendation: Not needed. When no heading exists, theme_block_props() does not add aria-labelledby, and the section remains a generic section without landmark labeling. This is acceptable for a content block - it's not a navigation or interactive region that requires labeling. Following the same pattern as ContentWithMedia.

designDecisions.question: When hasContent is false and ThemeHeading is hidden, should the inner blocks column span both columns?
designDecisions.recommendation: The current PHP template renders both grid columns even when ThemeHeading is hidden (the grid is still md:grid-cols-2). The inner blocks div simply takes its column and the empty space remains. This is acceptable behavior - if the user wants full-width content, they should use a different block. No change needed.
